name: Souletin Basque (Zuberera)

ancestors.notes: Souletin inherits the core Basque grapheme inventory from Batua but adds the unique grapheme ⟨ü⟩ for /y/ and has extensive phonological divergence due to French contact.

notes: Souletin (Zuberera/Xiberotarra). Easternmost Basque dialect, spoken in Soule/Zuberoa (Pyrénées-Atlantiques, France). The most phonologically divergent variety. Unique among Basque dialects for the front rounded vowel /y/ (written ⟨ü⟩, parallel to French), a fully functional aspirate /h/ in all positions, and aspirated stop allophones [pʰ, tʰ, kʰ] before vowels. Verb morphology is highly distinctive, including retention of archaic forms lost elsewhere. Extensive French phonological influence has reinforced the aspiration system and vowel inventory. Endangered, with an estimated few thousand active speakers.

sources.author: Hualde, J.I. & Ortiz de Urbina, J. (eds.)
sources.title: A Grammar of Basque

sources.author: Ladefoged, P. & Maddieson, I.
sources.title: The Sounds of the World's Languages
